Task: Assigns a target to one of predefined discrete categories
Output: A discrete label from a known reference set
Example: programmer ID, compiler family, malware family, cryptographic algorithm

Task: Groups similar instances into emergent clusters without labels
Output: A group label from an emergent partition

Task: Predicts whether a property is present
Output: A binary label or confidence score
Example: code similarity detection, vulnerability, maliciousness, patch presence, code removability, function or instruction start/end detection

Task: Identifies previously unknown candidates of interest for further validation
Output: A set of novel candidates for further validation
Example: unknown instance(s) of a known vulnerability class, crash-triggering input, symbolic path

Output: A graph, layout, or relational structure
Example: indirect call target, source-sink dependency, taint relation, value-set

Task: Recovers source-level semantics lost during compilation
Output: A recovered artifact approximating the orginal form
Example: variable type, function prototype, struct layout, function/variable symbol name, source-level pseudocode

Task: Reverses intentional concealment from code/data transformations
Output: A de-obfuscated or unpacked code snippet
Example: de-obfuscated or unpacked code snippet, decrypted string

Output: A condensed natural-language or structured description
